rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid and visible in the current scope before using them in assumptions or guarantees.
When AGREE reports 'named thing must be an expression with a type', trace the first invalid identifier on the same line and either resolve it or remove the clause.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must only reference visible AADL features or helper declarations within the same annex scope.
AGREE scalar type names must be int, real, or bool; avoid using package-qualified or undefined types.
When a type mismatch occurs, align the helper declaration type with the expression type (bool, int, or real).
Avoid introducing new identifiers or types that are not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports should be in component type scopes.
Implementation-level bindings must be in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
Use only visible AADL features or helper declarations in the same annex for AGREE expressions.
anti_patterns: Do not add new identifiers or types that are not defined in the AADL model or its configured dependencies.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
Do not attempt to fix a contract by adding new architecture; instead, resolve references or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about annex placement or assume/guarantee syntax
Model fusion or owner/scope preservation rules
